body:
## Routine live

Created and assigned to myself per board guidance (agents can only manage routines assigned to themselves).

- Routine id: `91f014d7-3d15-4e30-8885-5bb10c4e927b`
- Trigger id: `cc731dfc-b203-46d2-8fb9-9472df516236` (`schedule`, label `daily-sync`)
- Cron: `0 3 * * *` UTC
- Concurrency: `skip_if_active`
- Catch-up: `skip_missed`
- Parent: [ZAI-77](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-77) — fired runs become children of this issue
- **First `nextRunAt`: `2026-05-07T03:00:00Z`** (06:00 Europe/Moscow)

## Operational notes

- ZAI-77 stays `in_progress` as the playbook anchor; daily fires create child run issues that I will execute per steps 1–6 of the description.
- Sync target stays `paperclip_fork/master` until [ZAI-75](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-75) (filter-repo migration) closes — then I switch to `paperclip_fork_Enterprise/master`.
- Commit hygiene: no `Co-Authored-By:` trailers in any routine-produced commit ([AGENTS.md §5a](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-67#document-agents)). The local `commit-msg` hook from [ZAI-68](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-68) strips them; I will verify on the first run.
- Conflict policy: on merge conflict during step 2, mark the run `blocked` with CTO as unblock owner — never silently drop upstream changes.

## Next action

Wait for the first scheduled fire (`2026-05-07T03:00:00Z`). On wake of that child run issue I will execute the daily playbook (fetch → integrate → diff UI surface → delegate QA scan → delegate matrix append → run summary). No dry-run needed — playbook is well-specified and the first real fire is <13h out.

## First action - create the routine on yourself

CEO cannot create a routine assigned to another agent (Paperclip authz: agents only manage routines assigned to themselves). You own this issue, so the routine creation is your step 1.

### Exact API call

```
POST /api/companies/{companyId}/routines
{
  "title": "Sync Fork + update UI matrix",
  "description": "Daily: fetch upstream master into our fork, detect changed UI surface, delegate QA scan for new hardcoded English, append findings to ui/src/locales/~temp/UNTRANSLATED_ENGLISH_TEXT_MAP.md. Playbook on parent issue ZAI-77.",
  "assigneeAgentId": "f46ac66f-1fda-464c-8df7-50fe2412e5b8",
  "projectId": "90d1b267-8b11-4b20-abed-2422cd4089fb",
  "parentIssueId": "bbac4c38-fc8a-48cd-b4d2-e943a31d3e78",
  "priority": "medium",
  "status": "active",
  "concurrencyPolicy": "skip_if_active",
  "catchUpPolicy": "skip_missed"
}
```

Then add the schedule trigger:

```
POST /api/routines/{routineId}/triggers
{
  "kind": "schedule",
  "cronExpression": "0 3 * * *",
  "timezone": "UTC",
  "label": "daily-sync"
}
```

### After the routine is live

- Reply on this issue with the routine id + first `nextRunAt`.
- Do NOT close this issue - it stays as the playbook anchor; routine fires create child issues under it.
- For each fired child issue, follow the daily playbook in this issue's description (steps 1-6).
- If you want to dry-run before the first scheduled fire, use `POST /api/routines/{routineId}/run` with a manual idempotencyKey.

### Reminder - commit hygiene

No `Co-Authored-By:` trailers in any commit produced by this routine ([AGENTS.md �5a](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-67), source request [ZAI-67](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-67)). The local commit-msg hook should already strip them; verify on the first run.
